prompt:
Our report generator fans out ~40 fetches with raw goroutines and a WaitGroup, and when one fetch fails the rest keep burning API quota for nothing; worse, the error we surface is whichever one lost the race to write a shared variable (yes, really). I want an errgroup-shaped runner we own, in package group at the root of module go-group-runner, stdlib only — we cannot take the x/sync dependency in this repo.

API:
- New(parent context.Context) (*Group, context.Context) — the returned ctx is derived from parent and is the one every task gets.
- (*Group).Go(fn func(ctx context.Context) error) — runs fn in its own goroutine, passing that shared ctx.
- (*Group).Wait() error — blocks until EVERY task has returned (even ones that ignore cancellation), then reports the FIRST error that occurred; later errors must not overwrite it. Wait must also cancel the derived ctx before returning even when everything succeeded — we leaked timers through that hole once already.
- The moment any task returns a non-nil error, the shared ctx gets canceled so sibling tasks can bail out.
- Parent cancellation flows through to the tasks too.
- (*Group).SetLimit(n int) — optional bounded fan-out: at most n tasks running at once, Go blocks until a slot frees up. n < 1 is a programming error: panic. Calling SetLimit while tasks are in flight is also a panic (changing the limit mid-run is how you get heisenbugs).

Everything above gets exercised under -race by group_test.go (protected file — read it, never edit it), including a 100-goroutine error stampede. go test -race -timeout 30s ./... must pass.
